quickId: Tapered, fish-shaped body with metallic silver scales
Three long tail filaments, two long antennae
Active at night in bathrooms, basements, attics
habitatNotes: Surface grazing on starches, sizing in book bindings, wallpaper paste, stored paper.
management: Reduce humidity below 50%. Sticky monitors confirm activity. Long-lived; control is gradual.

quickId: About the size of a pinhead
Found on damp paper, behind wallpaper, in pantries
habitatNotes: Feeds on microscopic mold, not the paper itself. A moisture problem in disguise.
management: Dehumidify. The mold disappears, the booklice follow. No insecticide needed in most cases.
